# End-to-End Projects

<span class="part-kicker">Part VIII</span>

The projects turn the book into practice. Each project has a business problem, architecture, setup instructions, code walkthrough, tests, production hardening notes, and exercises.

## Project Sequence

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}

### [Project 1: Churn Prediction API](56-churn-prediction-api.qmd)

Train a baseline model on synthetic data, tune a threshold, serve predictions through FastAPI, and test the endpoint.
:::

::: {.project-card}

### [Project 2: Enterprise Document Q&A Assistant](57-enterprise-document-qa.qmd)

Build the book's running case study with ingestion, chunking, retrieval, citations, authentication placeholders, and evaluation.
:::

::: {.project-card}

### [Project 3: Agentic Support Ticket Triage](58-agentic-support-ticket-triage.qmd)

Classify tickets, retrieve policy, draft a response, decide escalation, require approval, and log tool use.
:::

::: {.project-card}

### [Project 4: Forecasting Service with Monitoring](59-forecasting-service-monitoring.qmd)

Use time series splits, a naive baseline, a forecast function, monitoring checks, and retraining triggers.
:::

:::

## How To Use The Projects

Run the tests before reading the walkthrough. Then change one behaviour at a time and rerun the tests. That practice is closer to production engineering than copying a full notebook in one sitting.

## Flow Back To The Book

- If the model behaves badly, return to Part I and Part II.
- If the endpoint contract is unclear, return to Part IV.
- If access control or auditability is unclear, return to Part V.
- If tools or approval gates are unclear, return to Part VI.
- If project ownership is unclear, return to Part VII.
